channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Senate advances nomination of Kash Patel, Trump's pick for FBI director
item.description: The Senate voted Tuesday along party lines to advance the nomination of Kash Patel, President Donald Trump's pick for FBI director, setting up a final vote on the Trump ally.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department and New York City mayor face judge's scrutiny in bid to dismiss criminal charges
item.description: Justice Department lawyers and New York City Mayor Eric Adams are set to face a federal judge who is signaling that he's unlikely to rubber stamp their request to drop corruption charges weeks before an April trial.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he's ordered firing of all remaining 'Biden-era' US attorneys
item.description: President Trump said that he has directed the Department of Justice (DOJ) to fire all remaining former President “Biden-era” U.S. attorneys, claiming the department “has been politicized like never before.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration halts support for representing unaccompanied children in immigration court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday stopped support for legal representation in immigration court for children who enter the United States alone, a setback for those fighting deportation who can't afford a lawyer.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE is getting lists of the military's probationary workers from the Pentagon
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Department of Government Efficiency staffers were at the Pentagon on Tuesday and receiving lists of the military’s probationary employees, U.S. officials said.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tens of millions of dead people aren't getting Social Security checks, despite Trump and Musk claims
item.description: The Trump administration is falsely claiming that tens of millions of dead people over 100 years old are receiving improper Social Security payments.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg’s philanthropy ends DEI programs, social advocacy efforts
item.description: FIRST ON FOX: Mark Zuckerberg’s philanthropy ended its Diversity, Equity and Inclusion programs Tuesday, along with its social advocacy efforts including immigration reform and racial equity grantmaking, Fox News Digital has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Howard Lutnick confirmed as Trump's commerce secretary
item.description: The Senate confirmed Howard Lutnick on Tuesday to serve as President Donald Trump’s secretary of commerce.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: First openly gay DC federal judge rakes Trump admin over military trans ban
item.description: A federal court on Tuesday heard arguments over whether the Trump administration has attempted to ban transgender service members from the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says Zelensky 'should have never started' war with Russia
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday appeared to blame Ukraine's leaders for the three-year war with Russia, arguing Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky "should have never started it.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kennedy says panel will examine childhood vaccine schedule after promising not to change it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — To earn the vote he needed to become the nation's top health official, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. made a special promise to a U.S. senator: He would not change the nation's current vaccination schedule. But on Tuesday, speaking for the first time to thousands of U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia, Ukraine take 'significant first step toward peace' after Rubio-led negotiations, White House insists
item.description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday he believes he can end the conflict between Russia and Ukraine after meetings between U.S. and Russian officials in Saudi Arabia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signs order to study how to expand IVF and calls for 'radical transparency' from government
item.description: President Donald Trump has signed an executive order to study how to expand access to in vitro fertilization and make it more affordable.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Congress closing in on shutdown deadline with no clear plan
item.description: Congress is struggling to strike a deal to keep the government funded as a looming deadline to prevent a shutdown next month gets closer. Lawmakers are less than a month away from a mid-March date to pass legislation to prevent a funding lapse — or risk the first shutdown in years.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Leonard Peltier leaves prison after Biden commuted his sentence in the killing of two FBI agents
item.description: Indigenous activist Leonard Peltier has been released from a high-security Florida prison. Peltier was freed Tuesday morning, nearly a month after then-President Joe Biden commuted his life sentence in the 1975 killings of two FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: 4 NYC deputy mayors resign after meeting with Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Four high-level officials in Mayor Eric Adams' administration resigned Monday after expressing concerns about the mayor's relationship with the Trump Administration, according to City Hall and sources familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, NewsNation

item.title: Black-owned businesses say DEI initiatives fell short
item.description: Trump’s rollback of DEI initiatives leaves some Black-owned business leaders unfazed. Biden’s DEI policies delivered some gains — but not enough to overcome structural barriers. ‘Good old boy’ network and other barriers shut minority-owned firms out of federal government contracts, some firms say.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Social Security head steps down over DOGE data access: Sources
item.description: King's exit from the administration is one of several departures of high-ranking officials concerned about DOGE staffers' potential unlawful access to private taxpayer information.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: As sports betting has soared, more people search online for help with gambling addiction
item.description: A study shows more people are looking for help to manage gambling addiction, in the years after a Supreme Court decision allowed online sports betting in 38 states.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle, MPR News

item.title: Republican introduces bill to make Trump's birthday a federal holiday
item.description: The Trump’s Birthday and Flag Day Holiday Establishment Act proposes the recognition of both Trump’s birthday and Flag Day, celebrated on June 14, as federal public holidays.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans hope to jam through defense, border funding boost ahead of shutdown fight
item.description: Senate Republicans have the ambitious goal of jamming through funding boosts for defense and border security — separately from the appropriations process — ahead of a mid-March deadline to prevent a government shutdown. Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: House, Senate standoff over Trump agenda escalates
item.description: The showdown between House and Senate Republicans is escalating as the two groups barrel ahead with their contrasting strategies to pass President Trump’s legislative agenda — with the upper chamber expected to move this week.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: For a St. Cloud family, Trump’s pause of refugee admissions brings worry for Afghan relatives
item.description: One of President Trump’s first actions after taking office was an executive order indefinitely suspending the U.S. refugee resettlement program. A family living in St. Cloud who fled Afghanistan a year ago worries about relatives left behind.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Health agencies lose staff members in key areas as Trump firings set in
item.description: As the dust settles from the first wave of firings at health agencies, here's how many people got cut and the impact of the roles that were lost.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: Former Vice President Kamala Harris to receive NAACP's Chairman's Award
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris will receive the NAACP's Chairman's Award during the civil rights organization’s Image Awards ceremony this weekend. The Chairman's Award honors individuals who excel in public service and leverage their unique platforms to ignite and drive meaningful change.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk’s DOGE seeks access to taxpayer data at IRS: AP sources
item.description: If successful, Musk and his group would have access to millions of tightly controlled files that include taxpayer information, bank records and other sensitive records.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Vivek Ramaswamy set to launch expected 2026 run for Ohio governor
item.description: The biotech entrepreneur would join Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost in the race to succeed term-limited Gov. Mike DeWine in the Buckeye State.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: NAACP spending guide urges Black Americans to avoid companies that dumped DEI
item.description: The nation’s oldest civil rights organization released its Black Consumer Advisory, a project intended to help educate Black Americans on “who’s pushing progress and who’s stuck in the past.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Activist Curtis Sliwa joins race for NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City mayoral race just got even more crowded. The founder of the Guardian Angels nonprofit, Curtis Sliwa, has officially filed paperwork to run for the city's top job. He also ran for mayor back in 2021 and lost to the current Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: White House limits AP access to Oval Office, Air Force One indefinitely
item.description: The White House says it will limit Associated Press journalists' access to the Oval Office and Air Force One, an escalation of a brooding conflict between the Trump administration and the wire service this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. confirmed as Trump's health secretary after close Senate vote
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as President Donald Trump’s health secretary, putting the prominent vaccine skeptic in control of $1.7 trillion in federal spending, vaccine recommendations and food safety as well as health insurance programs for roughly half the country.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Legislature OKs bill targeting high egg prices, allowing suspension of cage-free requirement
item.description: Nevada lawmakers on Wednesday gave final approval to a bill that could bring down the price of eggs, clearing the way for Gov. Joe Lombardo to give it his signature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
